items.headline: City approves Seeds of Peace Monument artwork at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for a new public artwork titled "Seeds of Peace," created by artist Mario Chiodo, to be installed at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument will become a permanent addition to the city's public art collection along the riverfront.

items.headline: Council adjusts Office of Mayor and City Council district office budgets
items.summary: The City Council proposed changes to Policy 1-18, Section 20, governing how the Office of the Mayor and City Council District Office budgets are handled during transition years. This affects how resources are allocated to council members' offices when staffing or leadership changes occur.

items.headline: City updates Health and Safety Element of General Plan
items.summary: Cupertino City Council is updating the Health and Safety Element of its General Plan (covering 2015–2040), which sets the framework for how the city addresses public health, emergency preparedness, and safety infrastructure. This update shapes long-term decisions on everything from disaster resilience to community health priorities.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution to appoint a permanent City Manager on May 19, 2026. This follows a formal process to fill the role on a permanent basis.

items.headline: New duplex permitted on Old Piedmont Road
items.summary: A permit has been issued for a new two-story duplex (2 units) at 2174 Old Piedmont Road, valued at over $1 million. This represents meaningful new housing supply in San José rather than the accessory unit conversions dominating recent permits.

items.headline: Sustainability Commission to review water shortage plans
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will hear an overview of Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. This matters because the contingency plan outlines how the city will manage water supply during droughts, directly affecting residents' water access and restrictions.

items.headline: Spring grants available for community events and neighborhoods
items.summary: The City Council will consider applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program's spring cycle on May 18. Residents and local organizations can learn which community projects and events received funding to improve neighborhood vitality.

items.headline: City Council to weigh new principles for residential electrification programs
items.summary: Palo Alto is developing proposed principles to guide residential electrification assistance programs designed to help the city comply with regional air quality regulations. The council will review the status update and proposed framework on May 15.
